title: Lastrea vellea
taxonomy: Lastrea vellea is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. Lastrea vellea is a type of epiphytic fern, meaning it grows on other plants or trees, and is native to Southeast Asia. The species is characterized by its delicate fronds and is often found in shaded areas. Lastrea vellea is classified as an evergreen fern, retaining its leaves throughout the year.
uses: Lastrea vellea, a type of fern, is known for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in reducing pain and inflammation. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a possible treatment for various infections. Additionally, Lastrea vellea is sometimes used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lastrea vellea for medicinal purposes.

preparations: Lastrea vellea, also known as Vellea fern, is a species of fern native to parts of Asia.
